{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mishine no Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mishine no Mikura.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Ujikan-cho 1, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 27 | Latitude Seconds = 18.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 28.7 |Map = Japan Mie |Label Position = bottom |deity = Mishine no Mikura no Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Ancient times |Main Hall = {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455222|N|136.724639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Mishine no Mikura''' (御稲御倉, also read as *Gotō no Mikura*<ref name="ss8">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 228</ref>) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|WD=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|WD=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. Originally, it was part of a complex of four granaries—Tsuki-no-Mikura, Mishio-no-Mikura, Fushiki-no-Mikura, and Mishine-no-Mikura—located west of the outer fence. By the mid-Heian period, these granaries, along with the Geheiden, were moved inside the outer sacred fence<ref>Fukuyama et al. (1975): pp.228–229</ref>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|WD=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Their attire consisted of the same sacred garments used during Naikū rituals, indicating the shrine's significance<ref name="gs"/>. The retrieved rice was milled, steamed, and served in sacred vessels ({{ill|ke|ja|笥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ke|1=ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11600004}}) before the deities<ref name="gs2"/>. The offerings were dedicated to Naikū, {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="gs2"/>.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), Mishine-no-Mikura was restored in the Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri style northwest of the main sanctuary and has since been rebuilt with each Shikinen Sengū<ref name="ss9"/>. After the Muromachi period, it was also known as the "Sacred Weaving Hall" (御機殿) due to its association with sacred weaving during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}<ref name="ss9"/>.
Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.
== Access ==
; To Ujibashi Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Proceed along the path from Ujibashi toward the main sanctuary of Naikū, then take the path leading west toward Aramatsuri-no-Miya, where Mishine-no-Mikura is enshrined<ref name="ic"/>. The nearby Geheiden can also be found here<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mishine no Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mishine no Mikura.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Ujikan-cho 1, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 27 | Latitude Seconds = 18.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 28.7 |Map = Japan Mie |Label Position = bottom |deity = Mishine no Mikura no Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Ancient times |Main Hall = {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455222|N|136.724639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Mishine no Mikura''' (御稲御倉, also read as *Gotō no Mikura*<ref name="ss8">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 228</ref>) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|WD=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|WD=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. Originally, it was part of a complex of four granaries—Tsuki-no-Mikura, Mishio-no-Mikura, Fushiki-no-Mikura, and Mishine-no-Mikura—located west of the outer fence. By the mid-Heian period, these granaries, along with the Geheiden, were moved inside the outer sacred fence<ref>Fukuyama et al. (1975): pp.228–229</ref>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|WD=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Their attire consisted of the same sacred garments used during Naikū rituals, indicating the shrine's significance<ref name="gs"/>. The retrieved rice was milled, steamed, and served in sacred vessels ({{ill|ke|ja|笥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ke|1=ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11600004}}) before the deities<ref name="gs2"/>. The offerings were dedicated to Naikū, {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="gs2"/>.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), Mishine-no-Mikura was restored in the Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri style northwest of the main sanctuary and has since been rebuilt with each Shikinen Sengū<ref name="ss9"/>. After the Muromachi period, it was also known as the "Sacred Weaving Hall" (御機殿) due to its association with sacred weaving during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}<ref name="ss9"/>.
Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.
== Access ==
; To Ujibashi Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Proceed along the path from Ujibashi toward the main sanctuary of Naikū, then take the path leading west toward Aramatsuri-no-Miya, where Mishine-no-Mikura is enshrined<ref name="ic"/>. The nearby Geheiden can also be found here<ref name="ic"/>.
== References ==

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|WD=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|WD=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. Originally, it was part of a complex of four granaries—Tsuki-no-Mikura, Mishio-no-Mikura, Fushiki-no-Mikura, and Mishine-no-Mikura—located west of the outer fence. By the mid-Heian period, these granaries, along with the Geheiden, were moved inside the outer sacred fence<ref>Fukuyama et al. (1975): pp.228–229</ref>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|WD=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Their attire consisted of the same sacred garments used during Naikū rituals, indicating the shrine's significance<ref name="gs"/>. The retrieved rice was milled, steamed, and served in sacred vessels ({{ill|ke|ja|笥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ke|1=ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11600004}}) before the deities<ref name="gs2"/>. The offerings were dedicated to Naikū, {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="gs2"/>.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), Mishine-no-Mikura was restored in the Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri style northwest of the main sanctuary and has since been rebuilt with each Shikinen Sengū<ref name="ss9"/>. After the Muromachi period, it was also known as the "Sacred Weaving Hall" (御機殿) due to its association with sacred weaving during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}<ref name="ss9"/>.
Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.
== Access ==
; To Ujibashi Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Proceed along the path from Ujibashi toward the main sanctuary of Naikū, then take the path leading west toward Aramatsuri-no-Miya, where Mishine-no-Mikura is enshrined<ref name="ic"/>. The nearby Geheiden can also be found here<ref name="ic"/>.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mishine no Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mishine no Mikura.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Ujikan-cho 1, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 27 | Latitude Seconds = 18.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 28.7 |Map = Japan Mie |Label Position = bottom |deity = Mishine no Mikura no Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Ancient times |Main Hall = {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455222|N|136.724639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Mishine no Mikura''' (御稲御倉, also read as *Gotō no Mikura*<ref name="ss8">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 228</ref>) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|WD=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|WD=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|WD=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. Originally, it was part of a complex of four granaries—Tsuki-no-Mikura, Mishio-no-Mikura, Fushiki-no-Mikura, and Mishine-no-Mikura—located west of the outer fence. By the mid-Heian period, these granaries, along with the Geheiden, were moved inside the outer sacred fence<ref>Fukuyama et al. (1975): pp.228–229</ref>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|WD=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Their attire consisted of the same sacred garments used during Naikū rituals, indicating the shrine's significance<ref name="gs"/>. The retrieved rice was milled, steamed, and served in sacred vessels ({{ill|ke|ja|笥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ke|1=ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11600004}}) before the deities<ref name="gs2"/>. The offerings were dedicated to Naikū, {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="gs2"/>.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), Mishine-no-Mikura was restored in the Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri style northwest of the main sanctuary and has since been rebuilt with each Shikinen Sengū<ref name="ss9"/>. After the Muromachi period, it was also known as the "Sacred Weaving Hall" (御機殿) due to its association with sacred weaving during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}<ref name="ss9"/>.
Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.
== Access ==
; To Ujibashi Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|WD=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Proceed along the path from Ujibashi toward the main sanctuary of Naikū, then take the path leading west toward Aramatsuri-no-Miya, where Mishine-no-Mikura is enshrined<ref name="ic"/>. The nearby Geheiden can also be found here<ref name="ic"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mishinenomikura]][[ja:御稲御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mishinenomikura]][[ja:御稲御倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:御稲御倉]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mishine no Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mishine no Mikura.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Ujikan-cho 1, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 27 | Latitude Seconds = 18.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 28.7 |Map = Japan Mie |Label Position = bottom |deity = Mishine no Mikura no Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Ancient times |Main Hall = {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455222|N|136.724639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Mishine no Mikura''' (御稲御倉, also read as *Gotō no Mikura*<ref name="ss8">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 228</ref>) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|qid=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|qid=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|qid=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|qid=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mishine no Mikura |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mishine no Mikura.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, Ujikan-cho 1, within the precincts of the Inner Shrine |Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 27 | Latitude Seconds = 18.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 | Longitude Minutes = 43 | Longitude Seconds = 28.7 |Map = Japan Mie |Label Position = bottom |deity = Mishine no Mikura no Kami |type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Ancient times |Main Hall = {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} |location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455222|N|136.724639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
'''Mishine no Mikura''' (御稲御倉, also read as *Gotō no Mikura*<ref name="ss8">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 228</ref>) is an auxiliary shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is also referred to as '''Mishine no Mikura no Kami''' (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p. 114</ref><ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): p. 16</ref><ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p. 156</ref>. The shrine is located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine, along the path leading to {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>.
Although small, it features the same {{ill|Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu Shimmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} architectural style as the Inner Shrine<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 56</ref>.
== Overview ==
Located in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, this shrine stands northwest of the main sanctuary (Shōgu) within the precincts of the {{ill|Naikū|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikū|1=Naikū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q23996212}}<ref name="gk"/>. It Ranks 6th among the 30 shrines under the jurisdiction of Naikū. Each time a festival is held at the main sanctuary of Naikū, a ritual is also conducted at the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/>. To the north of Mishine-no-Mikura is the {{ill|Geheiden|ja|外幣殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geheiden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ss8"/>.
The {{ill|Oryza sativa|en|Oryza sativa|qid=Q161426|lt=rice|1=Oryza sativa}} harvested from the sacred fields of Ise Jingu is presented as {{ill|nuiho|ja|抜穂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nuiho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/>. This rice is later retrieved in accordance with Naikū’s rituals and offered as {{ill|Ōmikue|ja|大御饌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmikue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. Essentially, it functions as the granary of Naikū<ref name="gk"/>. Following ancient customs, Shinto priests dressed in traditional garments deliver the rice to the Mikura<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.155</ref>.
The act of retrieving rice from the Mikura is called either {{ill|Gotō Hōge|ja|御稲奉下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotō Hōge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Gotō Hōge<ref name="gs"/>, Mishine Hōge<ref name="sy">Yano (2006): p.111</ref>). It is carried out by priests who have undergone purification rites the day before<ref name="gs"/>. After retrieval, the rice undergoes threshing, dehusking, and polishing to about 70%. It is then prepared into {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|qid=Q3313722|lt=sacred sake|1=Omiki}}, {{ill|Mochi|en|Mochi|qid=Q470519|lt=mochi|1=Mochi}}, and cooked rice ({{ill|Onii|ja|御飯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯|1=Cooked rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q523224}}) in the {{ill|Imibiya-den|ja|忌火屋殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibiya-den|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and offered before the deities<ref name="sy"/>.
Additionally, during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, one of the key rituals involves the placement of the central sacred pillar ({{ill|Shin-no-Mihashira|ja|心御柱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin-no-Mihashira|1=Q85880274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85880274}}), which is temporarily stored in Mishine-no-Mikura<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926070226/http://sankei.jp.msn.com/life/news/130925/trd13092522350017-n1.htm|title=Shin-no-Mihashira Ceremony at Naikū for Jingū Shikinen Sengū|publisher=Sankei Shimbun|work=MSN Sankei News|date=September 25, 2013|accessdate=October 13, 2013}}</ref>.
The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja|唯一神明造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明造|1=Shinmei-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q930545}} style<ref name="ic"/>. It faces east toward Naikū<ref name="ic"/>. The elevated structure with a {{ill|kirizuma-style|ja|切妻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kirizuma-style|qq=}} roof and decorative {{ill|chigi and katsuogi|ja|千木・鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chigi and katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q87220234}} resembles the East and West Treasuries of Naikū and the Geheiden<ref name="ss8"/>. Due to its elevated design, a removable staircase is attached during rice retrieval. The {{ill|Gon-negi|ja|権禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-negi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神職|1=Kannushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60523}} unlocks the door with a sacred key ({{ill|Mikagi|ja|御鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikagi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579157|1=Q135579157}}) to access the rice<ref>Yano (2006): pp.111–112</ref>. There are no surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fences or offering boxes.
== Enshrined deity ==
The enshrined deity is Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami (御稲御倉神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity serves as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of Mishine-no-Mikura<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. During the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, when the shrine is rebuilt, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami is temporarily transferred to the Geheiden<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2013): p.6</ref>.
In the Heian-period text "Daiji Mikagami," the deity is referred to as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, while in the Kamakura-period "Jinmyō Hisho," it is identified as {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}<ref name="gs P155-156">Sakurai (1970): pp.155–156</ref>.
== History ==
It is believed to have existed since ancient times<ref name="gs"/>. Originally, it was part of a complex of four granaries—Tsuki-no-Mikura, Mishio-no-Mikura, Fushiki-no-Mikura, and Mishine-no-Mikura—located west of the outer fence. By the mid-Heian period, these granaries, along with the Geheiden, were moved inside the outer sacred fence<ref>Fukuyama et al. (1975): pp.228–229</ref>. At the time, the structure was built in the {{ill|Azekura-zukuri|en|Azekura-zukuri|qid=Q2875112|lt=azekura-zukuri|1=Azekura-zukuri}} style without chigi and katsuogi<ref name="ss9">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p.229</ref>. As it was within the grounds of the main sanctuary, it was relocated with each Shikinen Sengū, but all four granaries were abolished by the end of the medieval period<ref name="ss9"/>.
The rice retrieval ceremony was conducted by four officials, including {{ill|negi|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11594490}} and Ōuchi-hito, accompanied by Ōmonoimi (great purification officials)<ref name="gs"/>. Their attire consisted of the same sacred garments used during Naikū rituals, indicating the shrine's significance<ref name="gs"/>. The retrieved rice was milled, steamed, and served in sacred vessels ({{ill|ke|ja|笥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ke|1=ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11600004}}) before the deities<ref name="gs2"/>. The offerings were dedicated to Naikū, {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja|滝deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takimatsuri-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="gs2"/>.
During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), Mishine-no-Mikura was restored in the Yuitsu-shinmei-zukuri style northwest of the main sanctuary and has since been rebuilt with each Shikinen Sengū<ref name="ss9"/>. After the Muromachi period, it was also known as the "Sacred Weaving Hall" (御機殿) due to its association with sacred weaving during the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}<ref name="ss9"/>.
Since the Meiji period, Mishine-no-Mikura-no-Kami has been formally enshrined, and in 1889, the shrine’s location was fixed along the path leading to Aramatsuri-no-Miya<ref name="ss9"/>.
== Access ==
; To Ujibashi Bridge * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=JR Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|qid=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway|1=Ise Expressway}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 23|en|Japan National Route 23|qid=Q5357579|lt=National Route 23 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 23}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Proceed along the path from Ujibashi toward the main sanctuary of Naikū, then take the path leading west toward Aramatsuri-no-Miya, where Mishine-no-Mikura is enshrined<ref name="ic"/>. The nearby Geheiden can also be found here<ref name="ic"/>.
